notes: before(str[,sep])/after(str[,sep]): sep is an arbitrary-length literal substring (not a single char, not a wildcard), defaulting to a single space if omitted or empty. Zero args -> empty string (same nfargs==0 sentinel pattern as words/first/rest); 1-2 args normal; 3+ errors 1 OR 2 ARGUMENTS. Only if sep ends up exactly a single space does str get leading/trailing-trimmed first; otherwise no trim at all. Plain literal substring search (String.indexOf(String) matches the C source's char-scan+strncmp exactly). index(list,sep,first,len): always exactly 4 args. sep's first character is used as-is with NO length validation (multi-char sep is not an error, silently uses the first char; empty sep defaults to space) -- unlike every other separator-taking function here. Positions into the ORIGINAL untrimmed list -- walking to the start of element `first` uses raw indexOf occurrences (no compression, even for space sep), but the resulting offset then gets an UNCONDITIONAL literal-space skip regardless of what sep is. Walking `len` more occurrences to find the end boundary is the same raw walk; if all len are found, trailing spaces before that cut point are also stripped -- but if the walk runs off the end first, the result is the raw untrimmed remainder to the end of the string (asymmetric with the success case, confirmed by the C source's control flow).

cases.input: index(a b | c d e | f gh | ij k,|,2,1)
index(a b | c d e | f gh | ij k,|,2,2)
index(a b | c d e | f gh | ij k,|,1,1)
